abstract: This paper provides a detailed analysis of cross-border cooperation networks within the framework of the Poland-Slovakia Cross-Border Cooperation Programme 2007–2013. Utilizing social network analysis (SNA), the study characterizes the structural properties of these partnerships by examining metrics such as network density, centrality, cohesion, and the positioning of specific intersection points between bridging and hub entities. The central objective is to determine the extent of integration between public and non-public institutions, as well as between urban centers, within this transnational space. Furthermore, the evaluation explores whether these cooperative structures lean toward a centralized model or function as decentralized micro-networks. By identifying key stakeholders and the strength of their organizational ties, the research offers insights into the effectiveness and configuration of collaborative regional development across the Polish-Slovak border.
